Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, suggesting significant renal impairment, possibly nephrotic syndrome or acute/chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones) further support renal dysfunction. Low HbA1c with normal glucose may indicate recent hypoglycemia or altered red cell turnover. Mild metabolic acidosis is present (low CO₂). Immediate nephrology evaluation is recommended for further assessment and management.
